meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Fix Version Control for Student Devs
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code to master version control and launch your first professional app today.
content.answer_block: Version control is the non-negotiable backbone of professional software development. By adopting a SaaS starter kit, you integrate Git-based versioning from day one. This approach automates your commit history, prevents catastrophic data loss, and ensures your portfolio projects are production-ready, modular, and instantly deployable for potential employers.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hours crafting the perfect logic, only to watch a rogue 'rm -rf' command or a corrupted file destroy your progress. For a student developer, the lack of version control isn't just an inconvenience; it’s a career-limiting bottleneck that turns your hard-earned code into fragile, ephemeral scripts.

But the era of manual backups is over. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you shift from 'writing code' to 'engineering systems.' This guide bridges the gap between amateur experimentation and professional architecture, equipping you with the tools to track every change and build software that actually scales.

steps.title: Initialize Repository Architecture
steps.description: Establish a clean Git foundation within your starter kit to ensure every iteration is tracked before you write a single line of feature code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a professional Git directory structure for a SaaS starter kit, including .gitignore for node_modules and environment variables.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial commit: Project structure'

steps.title: Automate Versioning with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to monitor your file changes and suggest semantic commit messages, turning your workflow into a documented history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a watch-script that triggers Claude Code to analyze pending changes and draft a conventional commit message.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'Refactor: Improve authentication module logic'

steps.title: Branching for Feature Isolation
steps.description: Learn to isolate new features into branches so your main build remains stable even when you are experimenting with complex AI integrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a workflow template for branching that separates core SaaS features from experimental UI components.

steps.title: Conflict Resolution Simulation
steps.description: Deliberately simulate a merge conflict to understand how to resolve discrepancies using Claude Code's contextual awareness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain how to resolve Git merge conflicts using Claude Code's diff analysis tools.

steps.title: Continuous Integration Setup
steps.description: Connect your repository to a CI pipeline that automates testing every time you push to your main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs unit tests on every push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always commit in small, atomic chunks; if you can't describe the change in one sentence, your commit is too large.
Use 'git stash' to save your work-in-progress when you need to switch branches to fix an urgent bug.
Treat your commit messages as technical documentation for your future self—six months from now, you will thank you.
Ignore your local build artifacts; a clean repository contains only source code and configuration files.
Leverage Claude Code to perform 'pre-commit' code reviews to catch security vulnerabilities before they hit your main branch.
content.next_steps: Once you have mastered versioning, deploy your starter kit to a platform like Vercel or Railway to see your code live in a production environment.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering with the precision of a senior developer. Your portfolio deserves more than a local folder; it deserves a professional history. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
